scenarios.title: Screen cracked from accidental drop — first ADH claim
scenarios.description: Customer dropped their iPhone 13 Mini on concrete, resulting in a shattered front display. No prior accidental damage claims have been made under the plan.

service_fee.notes: Standard ADH service fee applies for each incident

policy_references.snippet: If during the Plan Term you submit a valid claim notifying Apple that the Covered iPhone has failed due to accidental damage from handling ('ADH'), Apple will...repair the defect...subject to the service fee described below.
ADH coverage will expire...when Apple, as a result of ADH claims, has provided to you two (2) Service Events.

scenarios.reasoning: The cracked screen is the result of an unexpected, unintentional external event (drop), which is explicitly covered under section 3.2. The customer has not exceeded the two-incident ADH limit, and the standard $49 service fee applies.

scenarios.title: Power button fails to respond within plan term
scenarios.description: The power button on an iPhone 12 Pro Max stopped functioning after 14 months of normal use, with no signs of accidental damage or misuse.

policy_references.snippet: A defect in materials and workmanship has arisen in the Covered iPhone...Apple will either (a) repair the defect at no charge...or (b) exchange the Covered iPhone with a replacement product...

scenarios.reasoning: A non-functional power button is a defect in materials or workmanship, which is covered under section 3.1. No accidental damage is present, so no service fee applies and this does not count toward the ADH limit.
